title: bromus maximus subsp. gussonei
intro: Bromus maximus subsp. gussonei, also known as the tall oat-grass, is a subspecies of the perennial grass species Bromus maximus. It is a plant native to the Mediterranean region and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it beneficial for treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Bromus maximus subsp. gussonei include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, syrups, and salves for topical application.
